metadata.title: Heterotopic Ossification and Radiculopathy Following Transforaminal Lumbar Interbody Fusion with rhBMP-2: A Case Series Analysis
metadata.abstract: We conducted a study to determine the common characteristics of patients who developed radiculopathy symptoms and corresponding heterotopic ossification (HO) from transforaminal lumbar interbody fusions (TLIF) using recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2 (rhBMP-2). HO can arise from a disk space with rhBMP-2 use in TLIF. Formation of bone around nerve roots or the thecal sac can cause a radiculopathy with a consistent pattern of symptoms. We identified 38 patients (26 males, 12 females) with a mean (SD) age of 50.8 (7.5) years who developed radiculopathy symptoms and corresponding HO from TLIF with rhBMP-2 in the disk space between 2002 and 2015. To document this complication and improve its recognition, we recorded common patterns of symptom development and radiologic findings: specifically, time from implantation of rhBMP-2 to symptom development, consistency with side of TLIF placement, and radiologic findings. Radicular pain generally developed a mean (SD) of 3.8 (1.0) months after TLIF with rhBMP-2. Development of radiculopathy symptoms corresponded to consistent "pseudo-pedicle"-like HO. In all 38 patients, HO arising from the annulotomy site showed a distinct pseudo-pedicle pattern encompassing nerve roots and the thecal sac. In addition, development of radiculopathy symptoms and corresponding HO appear to be independent of amount of rhBMP-2. HO resulting from TLIF with rhBMP-2 in the disk space is a pain generator and a recognizable complication that can be diagnosed by assessment of symptoms and computed tomography characteristics.

spine_metadata.main_conclusion: Heterotopic ossification resulting from TLIF with rhBMP-2 in the disk space is a recognizable pain generator and complication characterized by a distinct pseudo-pedicle radiologic pattern that can be diagnosed through clinical symptom assessment and CT imaging.

pico.population: Patients with radiculopathy symptoms and heterotopic ossification following TLIF with rhBMP-2, mean age 50.8 years (range 2002-2015)
pico.intervention: TLIF with rhBMP-2 in disk space
pico.outcome: Time to radiculopathy symptom development, radiologic characteristics of HO, pattern of pseudo-pedicle formation

effect_measure.label: HO incidence independent of rhBMP-2 dose

chunks.content: We conducted a study to determine the common characteristics of patients who developed radiculopathy symptoms and corresponding heterotopic ossification (HO) from transforaminal lumbar interbody fusions (TLIF) using recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2 (rhBMP-2).
chunks.summary: Study objective was to characterize patients developing radiculopathy and heterotopic ossification following TLIF with rhBMP-2.

chunks.content: HO can arise from a disk space with rhBMP-2 use in TLIF. Formation of bone around nerve roots or the thecal sac can cause a radiculopathy with a consistent pattern of symptoms.
chunks.summary: Heterotopic bone formation around neural structures from rhBMP-2 in TLIF causes characteristic radiculopathy symptoms.

chunks.content: Study population: 38 patients (26 males, 12 females) with a mean (SD) age of 50.8 (7.5) years who developed radiculopathy symptoms and corresponding HO from TLIF with rhBMP-2 in the disk space between 2002 and 2015.
chunks.summary: Case series of 38 patients with HO and radiculopathy following rhBMP-2 TLIF over a 13-year period.

statistics.additional: N=38 (26 males, 12 females); mean age 50.8 ± 7.5 years; timeframe 2002-2015

chunks.content: To document this complication and improve its recognition, we recorded common patterns of symptom development and radiologic findings: specifically, time from implantation of rhBMP-2 to symptom development, consistency with side of TLIF placement, and radiologic findings.
chunks.summary: Study documented characteristic patterns of symptom timing, anatomic correlation, and radiologic findings associated with HO-related radiculopathy.

chunks.content: Radicular pain generally developed a mean (SD) of 3.8 (1.0) months after TLIF with rhBMP-2.
chunks.summary: Radiculopathy symptoms appeared approximately 3.8 months post-TLIF with rhBMP-2 use.

chunks.content: Development of radiculopathy symptoms corresponded to consistent "pseudo-pedicle"-like HO.
chunks.summary: Heterotopic ossification exhibits a characteristic pseudo-pedicle morphology correlating with symptom development.

chunks.content: In all 38 patients, HO arising from the annulotomy site showed a distinct pseudo-pedicle pattern encompassing nerve roots and the thecal sac.
chunks.summary: All 38 patients demonstrated identical pseudo-pedicle HO pattern originating from annulotomy site and encroaching on neural structures.

chunks.content: Development of radiculopathy symptoms and corresponding HO appear to be independent of amount of rhBMP-2.
chunks.summary: Heterotopic ossification and radiculopathy development were not dose-dependent with respect to rhBMP-2 amount.

chunks.content: HO resulting from TLIF with rhBMP-2 in the disk space is a pain generator and a recognizable complication that can be diagnosed by assessment of symptoms and computed tomography characteristics.
chunks.summary: Heterotopic ossification from rhBMP-2 TLIF is a definable complication with recognizable clinical and radiologic features serving as a pain generator.
